rag_rules: Place assume/guarantee declarations in component type scopes for interface behavior; use implementation scopes for assign clauses.
Use `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks to contain AGREE contract elements.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers and component ports in AGREE expressions only when they are explicitly declared in the same scope.
Define constants and equations in component type annexes when they describe interface behavior.
Place implementation-level bindings such as assignments in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE syntax uses `assume "label": expression;`, `guarantee "label": expression;`, `eq name : type = expression;`, and `const name : type = value;`.
AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level clauses like `assign` in component type scopes.
Do not reference AADL properties such as Compute_Execution_Time or unit tokens directly in AGREE expressions.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and their automatic scope in AGREE
Model fusion and architecture redesign during repair
